metric_name: IBM Lotus Notes DB2 integration outcome
metric_value: IBM acquired Lotus in June 1995 for $3.5B; Notes and DB2 integration deepened through late 1990s. Lotus Domino 5.0 (1999) provided tighter database integration. However Notes market share was eventually eroded by Microsoft Exchange. Product continues as HCL Notes after IBM sold to HCLSoftware in 2019.
notes: IBM Lotus acquisition confirmed; integration delivered but competitive outcome mixed. Prediction partially confirmed.

metric_name: DB2 will compete on equal basis with Oracle/Sybase/Informix for open systems
metric_value: Aberdeen predicted DB2 v2 had achieved functional parity and IS buyers should look at DB2 for distributed open production systems as serious Oracle/Sybase/Informix alternative
notes: Core thesis: obs-006, obs-007, obs-045

metric_name: DB2 competitive standing vs Oracle outcome
metric_value: DB2 revenue on UNIX/NT platforms grew 73% in 2000 (IBM Annual Report 2000), growing 3x faster than industry. ~1,000 companies replaced Oracle with DB2 in 18 months to 2000. SAP, Siebel, PeopleSoft selected DB2 as preferred database. However Oracle retained market leadership overall.
notes: Source: IBM Annual Report 2000. Prediction substantially confirmed for enterprise segment.
